observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Local Finance Committee to:
Analyze income trends from previous years to more accurately project the income for the upcoming year, ensuring that budget estimations are realistic and reflective of historical financial performance;
Adopt measures to eliminate the cash overdraft by exercising diligence, prudence, and proper control in the management of the Municipality’s funds. Limit expenditures to the total income realized and collected during the year and further reduce unnecessary operating expenditures until the cash overdraft is eliminated; and
Conduct a thorough analysis of both tax and non-tax revenues and identify strategies to generate additional revenue or income.
Finally, it was recommended and Management is agreeable to operate within its budget.

We recommended and Management concurred to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Accounts Payable balance by recording the unreported obligations and the corresponding asset/expense accounts to ensure that the financial statements reflect a more accurate and complete picture of the municipality's financial position and compliance with applicable accounting standards; and
Direct the Local Finance Committee to closely monitor the LGU’s expenditures to prevent overspending and avoid similar issues in the future. This proactive oversight will help ensure proper financial management and the timely recording of all obligations.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Human Resource Officer to clearly define the duties and responsibilities of Job Order (JO) personnel in their contracts. Additionally, ensure that accomplishment reports accurately reflect the tasks performed and align with the specified duties;
Establish a standardized wage rate for Job Order (JO) personnel based on their qualifications and job suitability, taking into account the Municipality's financial capacity and the guidelines set by the Regional Tripartite Wages and Productivity Board (RTWPB); and
If budget constraints exist, assess the necessity of hiring Job Order (JO) personnel and determine the required number to meet the LGU's operational needs, prioritizing critical roles and aligning staffing with financial capacity.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that at the end of each year, the Municipal Treasurer transfers all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability - DRRM”;
Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary journal entries to transfer the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the special trust account, following the suggested entries:
Justify the retention of ₱1,957,422.39 in the 5% LDRRMF despite being declared as unutilized and bound to be transferred to the STF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local School Board:
Stop the practice of using the SEF for purposes other than those which are within the allowable Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) outlined in the aforementioned Act, Circulars, and other relevant statutory guidelines;
Review and assess existing programs and projects to ensure a more strategic and efficient allocation of resources, prioritizing the incorporation of mandated programs and activities; and
Ensure that, in the preparation of the next SEF budget, only those PPAs explicitly permitted under the SEF guidelines are included for funding.
Finally, we recommend that Management promptly submit the missing official receipts or sales invoices as indicated in the audit remarks column.
Require the Accounting Office and the Property Custodian to reconcile their respective records regularly for check and balance;

Management comment:
The Management commented that they had already done with the physical count of office equipment, however, due to limited manpower available in the GSO and the safety measures adopted during the COVID-19 pandemic, the physical count for infra is not yet finished.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
The Management must augment the staffing of personnel in the GSO to fulfill its mandate such as, among others, the conduct of the physical count of PPEs and inventories for reconciliation purposes in the accounting records.  Thus, the check and balance is achieved which is a sound system of internal control.

b. Apply perpetual inventory method and the accounting standards and policies of recording acquisition of supplies and materials and subsequent recognition of expense consistently in accordance with Sections 114 and 115 of Chapter 7, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs and Item 35 of PPSAS 12–Inventories;

c. Apply perpetual inventory method and the accounting standards and policies of recording acquisition of supplies and materials and subsequent recognition of expense consistently in accordance with Sections 114 and 115 of Chapter 7, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs and Item 35 of PPSAS 12–Inventories;

d. Prepare supplies ledger cards for every procured inventory item for reconciliation with the records of the GSO and reference for computing the items issued using the perpetual inventory method; 

Likewise, we have recommended instructing the General Services Officer (GSO) to:

Prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) apart from the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) which is to be consolidated weekly and submit both documents to the Accounting Office to serve as a basis to compute for the cost of supplies and materials issued and ending inventory using the moving average method; and

Coordinate with the Accounting Office to reconcile its inventory records and conduct regular physical count to come up with the accurate inventory balance as of the reporting date.
observations.reason: Perpetual inventory  method has already been applied, however, preparation of the Supply Ledger Cards (SLC), Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) still not yet practiced.

observations.managementAction: No action yet taken by Management

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Office to:
Conduct verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the status of the concerned accountable officer/employee/recipient whether still alive or displaced;
Issue demand letters to the persons concerned; and
After exhausting efforts and the necessary means for the collection of receivables, request for the write-off of these accounts along with the necessary supporting documents under Section 8.3.b of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts and make necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant once constructed projects are completed and furnish the latter with the documents for proper reclassification to the appropriate accounts; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the reconciliation of records to determine the status and correct balances of CIP accounts.
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify the completed projects to the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;
Recognize Accounts Payable for completed projects with unpaid final billing to close the CIP accounts;
Compute the required depreciation on the completed projects and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts; and
Identify those projects, if any, which were completed in the prior years that are still included in the CIP accounts and reclassify to the same to their appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and other concerned officials to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents to COA for post-audit pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to           (a) submit immediately the complete plans with details of the 300 cubic meter resevoir, perimeter fence and water treatment plant shed house, as required by the COA-10 TSO; and (b) submit request of technical inspection of this project along with the necessary documents needed pursuant to Section 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that Management (a) review budget operations and strictly implement the rules and regulations mandated by the manual to avoid irregular and illegal disbursements by the LGU and (b) submit copies of the Certification of Funds Actually Available and Certification of Savings duly signed by the Local Treasurer and Local Accountant.

We recommended that Management (a) instruct the Accounting Office to maintain subsidiary ledgers for Loans Payable- Domestic as well those other controlling account appearing in the General Ledger as provided under Sections 111(1) and Section 114(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to ascertain the accuracy and correctness of its balances (b) trace back possible errors in the recording of transactions affecting the Loans Payable-Domestic account and effect adjustment through a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to have a reconciled balance with the bank record.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to set up Real Tax Receivable and Special Tax Receivable for CY 2018 and the years thereafter as provided under Section 20, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual (b) Enforce collection of the unpaid delinquent Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to avoid significant amount of tax delinquencies.
observations.reason: JEV was already drawn to recognize the receivable
The concerned officer submitted the required documents on February 2020, however, these are still subject to evaluation from the technical services office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) establish programs and activities to be funded by the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund for additional disaster preparedness and mitigation; and (b) ensure proper utilization of the fund, bearing in mind the purpose and documentary requirements upon disbursement.
observations.reason: The 2019 Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) already included PPAs for special trust fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to strictly observe the deadline on the submission of all paid vouchers, payrolls, JEVs and other financial reports pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009–006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: This audit observation is reiterated in the CY 2019 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct its Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) days at the end of the semester to avoid sanctions of noncompliance and likewise instruct the same to furnish a copy of the PMRs duly acknowledged by the GPPB to the Office of the Auditor for review and evaluation.

d. Prepare supplies ledger cards for every procured inventory item for reconciliation with the records of the GSO and reference for computing the items issued using the perpetual inventory method; 

Likewise, we have recommended instructing the General Services Officer (GSO) to:

Prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) apart from the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) which is to be consolidated weekly and submit both documents to the Accounting Office to serve as a basis to compute for the cost of supplies and materials issued and ending inventory using the moving average method; and

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Management agreed to strictly comply with the prescribed documentary requirements and see to it that complete supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and likewise advise the Municipal Accountant to go over the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012–001 dated June 14, 2012 in a particular government transaction and instruct her not to accept and process disbursement vouchers without the necessary supporting documents as required by said Circular.
observations.managementAction: Some lacking documents are already submitted

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Management agreed directing its Bids and Awards Committee to:

See to it that the date of receipt of the winning bidder is duly written in the Notice to Proceed to ensure enforceability of contracts;
Require the winning bidder to post a warranty security in accordance with Section 62.2.3.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 in case of structural defects; and

In relation to the compliance with the warranty security, include the same along with the as-built plans in documenting the final payment of its infrastructure projects in accordance with Item 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management effect an adjustment on the over-statement of RATA through reimbursement of the amount overpaid and that for the ensuing months, RATA shall be pro-rated based on the actual work / attendance of the official in accordance with the above-mentioned Circular. 

We also recommended that payment for RATA should be paid separate from the monthly salary, and a Certification by the Secretary of the Sanggunian for their attendance to session and another Certification by the Human Resource Management Office for No Vehicle Assigned by the Government should be attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) instruct the Accounting Office to maintain subsidiary ledgers for Loans Payable- Domestic as well those other controlling account appearing in the General Ledger as provided under Sections 111(1) and Section 114(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to ascertain the accuracy and correctness of its balances (b) trace back possible errors in the recording of transactions affecting the Loans Payable-Domestic account and effect adjustment through a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to have a reconciled balance with the bank record.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to set up Real Tax Receivable and Special tax Receivable for CY 2018 and the years thereafter as provided under Section 20, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual (b) Enforce collection of the unpaid delinquent Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to avoid significant amount of tax delinquencies.

We recommended that management exhaust all efforts to identify the composition of these balances and perform the necessary procedures or adjustments to reconcile amounts as it would affect the fair presentation of the Municipal Government’s Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management (a) establish programs and activities to be funded by the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund for additional disaster preparedness and mitigation; and (b) ensure proper utilization of the fund, bearing in mind the purpose and documentary requirements upon disbursement.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to strictly observe the deadline on the submission of all paid vouchers, payrolls, JEVs and other financial reports pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009–006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Management direct its Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) days at the end of the semester to avoid sanctions of noncompliance and likewise instruct the same to furnish a copy of the PMRs duly acknowledged by the GPPB to the Office of the Auditor for review and evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We hereby recommended that Management direct the municipal officials to strictly adhere to Section 1.2.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012–001 dated June 14, 2012 and Item 5 of COA Circular No. 97–002 dated February 10, 1997 as regards liquidation of cash advances for local travel to avoid sanctions of noncompliance and submit revised itineraries of travel on those previously approved itineraries not followed by indicating the actual time, destination, and costs incurred to conform with the liquidation reports and duly accomplished certificates of travel completed indicating the reasons of such deviations.

We have also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant not to receive those liquidations on cash advances with incomplete documentation and withhold the salary of the officials concerned until the full liquidation of the said cash advances.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, there no outstanding cash advances, except for the CA for CIF totaling P200,000.00

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Management agreed to strictly comply with the prescribed documentary requirements and see to it that complete supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and likewise advise the Municipal Accountant to go over the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012–001 dated June 14, 2012 in a particular government transaction and instruct her not to accept and process disbursement vouchers without the necessary supporting documents as required by said Circular.
observations.managementAction: Some lacking documents are already submitted
observations.reason: There are lacking documents still up for submission

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Management agreed directing its Bids and Awards Committee to:

See to it that the date of receipt of the winning bidder is duly written in the Notice to Proceed to ensure enforceability of contracts;
Require the winning bidder to post a warranty security in accordance with Section 62.2.3.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 in case of structural defects; and

In relation to the compliance with the warranty security, include the same along with the as-built plans in documenting the final payment of its infrastructure projects in accordance with Item 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: No submission yet of warranty security pending payment of the final billing

observations.recommendation: For the succeeding projects to be implemented, we have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct its Bids and Awards Committee to post the Notices of Award, copies of the duly perfected contract, and Notices to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website to maintain transparency and adequate disclosure of the procurement transactions of the Municipality and likewise submit a certification from the Head of the BAC Secretariat that such notices were posted in conspicuous places of the Municipality for contract review purposes.

We recommended that Management direct the supply officer concerned to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 77–61 regarding fuel consumption and submit the driver’s trip tickets to the Office of the Auditor and likewise prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travel and instruct the same to prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in its prescribed format in accordance with Annex G of the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption and submit within 10 days of the succeeding month to the Office of the Auditor as further prescribed by Section 361 of GAAM for proper monitoring and control of fuel–related expenditures.

We hereby recommended that Management direct the municipal officials concerned to strictly adhere to sound internal control as prescribed by Section 2.2 of COA Circular No 97–002, Sections 10 and 48 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and Items II–A and II–B of the BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 and likewise encourage the BAC to establish an adequate and plausible control measure to hasten the consolidation of the necessary documentary requirements to immediately process payment to its supplier of fuel and avoid resorting to procuring on reimbursement basis.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursement of fuel was discontinued

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management effect an adjustment on the over-statement of RATA through reimbursement of the amount overpaid and that for the ensuing months, RATA shall be pro-rated based on the actual work / attendance of the official in accordance with the above-mentioned Circular. 

We also recommended that payment for RATA should be paid separate from the monthly salary, and a Certification by the Secretary of the Sanggunian for their attendance to session and another Certification by the Human Resource Management Office for No Vehicle Assigned by the Government should be attached.

We recommended that Management create an inventory committee to conduct a physical inventory on the municipality’s properties in compliance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS.

We also recommended creating an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to appraise the value of said properties and pre-pare a report thereon. Of which, the report should be classified for each type of Property, Plant and Equipment for purposes of comparison / re-conciliation with the general ledger balances; and that the condition of the properties should be indicated to determine those unserviceable / obsolete and assets still serviceable but no longer being used.

Furthermore, we recommended to require the same Appraisal and Disposal Committee to work on the disposal of the un-serviceable properties to be dropped from the books.

Likewise, we recommended Management to instruct the Accounting Department to maintain Property Ledger Cards of all Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality and must be kept updated to facilitate reconciliation of ac-counting records with the Inventory Report.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of pertinent reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines and regulations for the utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Management require the Municipality’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procure-ment Monitoring Re-port to be approved by the head of the pro-curing entity and sub-mit the same to the Government Procure-ment Policy Board in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days af-ter the end of each se-mester and furnish a copy to the office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management strictly follow the Procurement Guidelines especially involving large amounts. PCF should be spent solely for emergencies and unforeseen expenses and not on regular expenses.

We recommended that Management effect an adjustment on the overstatement of RATA through reimbursement of the amount overpaid and that for the ensuing months, RATA shall be pro-rated based on the actual work / attendance of the official in accordance with the abovementioned Circular.

We also recommended that payment for RATA should be paid separate from the monthly salary, and a Certification by the Secretary of the Sanggunian for their attendance to session and another Certification by the Human Resource Management Office for No Vehicle Assigned by the Government should be attached.

We recommended that Management, starting January 1, 2017 (effectivity of GAA 2017), strictly comply with the provisions under Section 47 of RA 10924 otherwise known as The General Appropriations Act of 2017 which provides that in no case shall deductions be made to reduce the employee’s monthly net take home pay to an amount lower than Four Thousand Pesos (P4,000.00).

We recommended that Management create an inventory committee to conduct a physical inventory on the municipality’s properties in compliance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS.

We also recommended creating an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to appraise the value of said properties and prepare a report thereon. Of which, the report should be classified for each type of Property, Plant and Equipment for purposes of comparison / reconciliation with the general ledger balances; and that the condition of the properties should be indicated to determine those unserviceable/obsolete and assets still serviceable but no longer being used.

Furthermore, we recommended to require the same Appraisal and Disposal Committee to work on the disposal of the unserviceable properties to be dropped from the books.

Likewise, we recommended Management to instruct the Accounting Department to maintain Property Ledger Cards of all Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality and must be kept updated to facilitate reconciliation of accounting records with the Inventory Report.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions set forth in RA No. 7160 and DBM Local Budget Circular no. 98 observing the 55% PS limitation. Provide an explanation and / or justification on why the actual personal services incurred exceeded the 55% PS limitation.

We also recommended that Management establish a plausible scheme on generating more revenues and / or reduce its PS expenses.

Lastly, we recommended to stop granting additional benefits and / or extra cash gifts in excess of the PS limitation to avoid disallowances.
We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines and regulations for the utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
We recommended that Management should carefully record the Agency’s transactions to avoid future errors and to provide proper monitoring of the affected Funds and Accounts.
We recommended that Management prepare and submit the monthly reports as required by the COA Circular 2012–002 regarding utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and submit the same to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format in accordance with the specified time frame stipulated by the law and also furnish a copy to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended the Agency to review and observe the proper procedures stipulated in Republic Act 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regula-tions. Noncompliance of the provisions may be subject to penalties and sanctions under appropriate laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Proper procurement process

observations.recommendation: We recommended Ma-nagement to explain / justify how these ex-penses were related to the projects provided for under the 20% De-velopment Fund.
observations.managementAction: Realignment of budget for the said expenses
observations.reason: Several transac-tions disbursed during the first quarter

observations.recommendation: We recommended Ma-nagement to seek ex-planation and / or jus-tification from the res-ponsible and accoun-table officials con-cerned, instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and main-tain separate subsidi-ary ledger, books of accounts and trial ba-lance for the 20% De-velopment Fund and Other Special Funds in order to provide ade-quate information, monitoring and con-trol as to its proper utilization and imple-mentation in conso-nance with Section 13, 105 & 107 of Manual on NGAS.

We recommended the Agency to justify the inconsistencies of the dates of purchase and delivery of the sup-plies. We also recom-mend strict adherence to the procurement process and to attach complete documen-tary requirements up-on submission of pur-chase orders.
observations.managementAction: Management justification

observations.recommendation: We recommended pro-viding justification for the inclusion of those programs of activities in the GAD Budget. Observe the guidelines in conducting activi-ties specifically for the purpose of GAD man-dated by the existing laws and regulations
observations.managementAction: Management justification

observations.recommendation: We recommended Ma-nagement to disconti-nue the charging of inappropriate expenses and strictly observe the guidelines and pro-visions under RA 7160 and NGAS for the proper utilization of the Special Educa-tion Fund.
